definition: Broad doctrinal formula stating the impermanent character of conditioned things.
notes: Use this record for the full formula line when the conditioned side of the family is foregrounded explicitly. SN 43.1 anchors the line itself. It keeps saṅkhata tied to impermanence without collapsing back into created-language, and the OSF glossary supports keeping the family in conditioned-language rather than created-language. This gives translators a governed line when the broad conditioned category is the point rather than the more specific saṅkhārā family.

context_rules.context: When surrounding prose is more analytic than concrete
context_rules.notes: Controlled alternate for more analytic English.

example_phrases.notes: Keep the conditioned side explicit and avoid created-language.

authority_basis.scope: Supports the canonical formula line itself.
Supports keeping saṅkhata in conditioned-language rather than created-language.

translation_policy.default_scope: Use as the standard rendering when the broad conditioned category is the point of the formula.
translation_policy.when_not_to_apply: Do not replace conditioned with created, and do not use this broader line when the more specific saṅkhārā formulas are actually in view.
translation_policy.drift_risk: Prevents drift between saṅkhata and saṅkhārā in translator-facing formula work.
